message:
test(readme): IndexLabel variant coverage (Utf8 / Datetime64 / Timedelta64)

IndexLabel has 4 variants but only Int64 was exercised by integration
tests. The 3 typed-temporal variants are core pandas surface (string
indexes for grouped data, datetime indexes for time series, timedelta
indexes for durations).

Adds readme_index_label_variants_round_trip — reachable via prelude
alone — that constructs a Series with each variant and asserts the
labels round-trip through the index:

- IndexLabel::Utf8 ("alpha", "beta", "gamma") with Int64 values
- IndexLabel::Datetime64 (ns since epoch) with Float64 values
- IndexLabel::Timedelta64 (1d, 2d, 3d via Timedelta::NANOS_PER_DAY)
  with Int64 values

Each path verifies series construction succeeds + the first label
matches the expected variant via a pattern match.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.35.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.33","title":"test(readme): AlignMode + DateOffset + date_range/bdate_range functional coverage","description":"Self-review caught these uncovered surfaces:

DateOffset: 3 variants (Day, BusinessDay, MonthEnd). Only DateOffset::Day(1) is named in the prelude completeness guard — none are functionally driven via apply_date_offset.

date_range / bdate_range: in the prelude since fd90.261 / fd90.269 but never called functionally.

 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.34","title":"test(readme): NullKind::NaT + IntervalClosed::Both/Neither variant coverage","description":"Self-review caught:

NullKind: 3 variants (Null, NaN, NaT). NaN and Null tested; NaT untested. NaT is the timestamp-specific null marker (datetime/timedelta).

IntervalClosed: 4 variants (Left, Right, Both, Neither). Left and Right tested; Both and Neither untested. These have observable behavior on the contains() method:
- Both: '[a, b]' — both endpoints inclusive
- Neither: '(a, b)' — both endpoints exclusive

Plan: extend readme_pandas_helpers_round_trip with assertions for IntervalClosed::Both and ::Neither contains() behavior at endpoints. @@ -3712,6 +3712,62 @@ fn readme_serialization_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Er
     Ok(())
 }
 
+/// fd90.35: IndexLabel variant coverage. IndexLabel has 4 variants
+/// (Int64, Utf8, Timedelta64, Datetime64) but only Int64 was exercised
+/// by integration tests. The 3 typed-temporal variants are core
+/// pandas surface.
+#[test]
+fn readme_index_label_variants_round_trip() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    // ── IndexLabel::Utf8 ────────────────────────────────────────
+    let str_labels: Vec<IndexLabel> =
+        vec!["alpha".into(), "beta".into(), "gamma".into()];
+    let s_utf8 = Series::from_values(
+        "tag",
+        str_labels.clone(),
+        vec![Scalar::Int64(1), Scalar::Int64(2), Scalar::Int64(3)],
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(s_utf8.len(), 3);
+    // Index labels survive as Utf8 variants.
+    assert!(matches!(
+        s_utf8.index().labels()[0],
+        IndexLabel::Utf8(ref s) if s == "alpha"
+    ));
+
+    // ── IndexLabel::Datetime64 ──────────────────────────────────
+    let dt_labels: Vec<IndexLabel> = vec![
+        IndexLabel::Datetime64(1_700_000_000_000_000_000),
+        IndexLabel::Datetime64(1_700_086_400_000_000_000),
+    ];
+    let s_dt = Series::from_values(
+        "ts_value",
+        dt_labels,
+        vec![Scalar::Float64(100.0), Scalar::Float64(200.0)],
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(s_dt.len(), 2);
+    assert!(matches!(
+        s_dt.index().labels()[0],
+        IndexLabel::Datetime64(_)
+    ));
+
+    // ── IndexLabel::Timedelta64 ─────────────────────────────────
+    let td_labels: Vec<IndexLabel> = vec![
+        IndexLabel::Timedelta64(Timedelta::NANOS_PER_DAY),
+        IndexLabel::Timedelta64(2 * Timedelta::NANOS_PER_DAY),
+        IndexLabel::Timedelta64(3 * Timedelta::NANOS_PER_DAY),
+    ];
+    let s_td = Series::from_values(
+        "lag",
+        td_labels,
+        vec![Scalar::Int64(10), Scalar::Int64(20), Scalar::Int64(30)],
+    )?;
+    assert_eq!(s_td.len(), 3);
+    assert!(matches!(
+        s_td.index().labels()[0],
+        IndexLabel::Timedelta64(_)
+    ));
+    Ok(())
+}
+
 /// fd90.33: AlignMode + DateOffset + date_range/bdate_range functional
 /// coverage. Existing tests only exercised AlignMode::Outer and the
 /// DateOffset::Day variant in name-only form. date_range/bdate_range
